commits.subject: Prove pole-divisor purity (Milne 3.3 Substep 4a): on a locally Noetherian integral scheme with regular stalks, $h\in K(X)\setminus\mathcal O_{X,P}$ has a pole at a coheight-1 generization $z\rightsquigarrow P$. Serre-free/UFD-free route: elementary swap-pair computation ($b(ut-vs)=0$) replaces normality of regular local rings; associated prime of $A/(b)$ over $R_\mathfrak p$ + Krull height transfer under localization. New Albanese/PolePurity.lean (617 LOC, axiom-clean incl. scheme-level exists_specializes_coheight_eq_one_of_notMem_stalk_range); blueprint node lem:pole_divisor_purity wired into lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy's proof; Milne 3.3 plan comment updated (remaining: substeps 1-3 difference-map machinery, 4b diagonal intersection).
blueprint(Albanese): fix lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy header — restore optional-arg title and \leanok ordering (statement matches Lean; proof correctly unmarked for the remaining Milne 3.3 sorry)
